LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, AND, DIANE LEUNG MOTION TO DECLARE PLAINTIFF A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT; REQUEST FOR PREFILING ORDER AND ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: REQUEST FOR PRE-FILING ORDER DENIED. (THERE WAS ALREADY A PRE-FILING ORDER ISSUED BY THE COURT IN CASE NUMBER 467638, FILED ON 12-5-2007.) MOTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE IS NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY PREVAIL IN THE PRESENT LITIGATION. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO POST $2,000.00 SECURITY WITH THE COURT, TO BE POSTED BY APRIL 20, 2009. MATTER IS STAYED UNTIL THE AMOUNT IS POSTED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5759
